title: Savory Uruguayan Lentils Bowl
description: A light, chewy lentil bowl infused with sherry, pickled cucumber, and aromatic spices for a savory Uruguayan twist.

ingredients.name: Dried green lentils
ingredients.notes: Rinse and soak 20 minutes, then drain

instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 20 minutes, then drain.
Heat the olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat and add the smoked paprika and cumin, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the lentils to the saucepan and stir to coat them with the spices.
Pour in the 2 cups of water and add salt and pepper; bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, partially covered, for 25–30 minutes, or until the lentils are tender yet chewy.
While the lentils are cooking, gently warm the sherry in a small saucepan until it simmers.
When the lentils are almost done, stir in the warm sherry and continue simmering for an additional 5 minutes to reduce the liquid slightly.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and fold in the diced pickled cucumber, lemon zest, and chopped parsley.
Taste the bowl and adjust seasoning with extra salt or pepper if needed.
Divide the cooked lentils into bowls and serve immediately, optionally garnishing with a sprinkle of extra parsley.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
